pi_items.source: verifier.py checks for DASHBOARD_NAME = 'GOC Standard Units'
verifier.py uses _find_graph_by_title to strictly enforce these exact titles
Matches the explicit scenario described in the task prompt
Task utilizes real AWS CloudWatch, EC2, and load balancer metrics
Defined in export_result.sh and consumed via RESULT_PATH in verifier.py

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real-world Numenta Anomaly Benchmark (NAB) dataset, populated into Graphite via feed_real_data.py using real AWS EC2 metrics. A significant contradiction exists in the task prompt: it incorrectly claims that servers.datacenter.machine_temperature is in Celsius and instructs the agent to apply a scale(1.8) and offset(32) conversion to Fahrenheit. Analysis of the original machine_temperature_system_failure.csv dataset reveals its values range from 2.08 to 108.51, and its sister dataset ambient_temperature_system_failure.csv features standard room temperatures around 70, confirming the raw data is already natively in Fahrenheit. The verifier rigidly enforces this flawed mathematical conversion. All other metadata parameters concerning the dashboard configuration accurately align with task scripts.
